title: Floral Thai Steak Skewers
description: These crunchy Thai steak skewers combine floral notes with a monsoon-inspired sweetness and are sugar-free-friendly for a delightful summer meal.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup (60ml) soy sauce
ingredients.notes: can be mixed with a bit of lemon juice for added brightness

ingredients.name: 3 cloves garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: ensure you don’t overmince to prevent bitterness

ingredients.name: 2 tsp fish sauce
ingredients.notes: adjust based on your preference for saltiness

ingredients.name: 1 tbsp honey or maple syrup
ingredients.notes: use sparingly to maintain sugar-free status

ingredients.name: 2-inch piece fresh lemongrass, minced
ingredients.notes: trim the tough outer layer and use only the inside white part

ingredients.name: Pinch of red pepper flakes
ingredients.notes: for some heat, adjust to taste

instructions: Combine soy sauce, fish sauce, lime juice, honey/maple syrup, minced garlic, lemongrass, and water in a blender or food processor. Blend until smooth.
Pour the marinade over the skirt steak slices. Place on skewers and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, turning occasionally.
Preheat grill to medium-high heat (400°F). Thread the marinated steak onto metal skewers, ensuring they are secure so that they do not fall off during grilling.
Grill the skewers until well-charred on all sides and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 135°F for medium-rare), about 12-15 minutes. Avoid burning by keeping an eye on them as they cook.
Remove from heat, allow to rest for a few minutes before serving with lime wedges, fresh herbs, or your choice of dipping sauces.
